channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Trump administration eases rules on some marijuana categories. Here's what to know
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said he is immediately moving medical marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III, which includes drugs like ketami...

item.title: A global fertility reversal is unfolding, and it could upend who becomes parent in decades ahead
item.description: With few exceptions, birth rates are falling worldwide. What does this mean? Put simply, the fertility rate describes the average number of children a...

item.title: How to treat chronic pain and depression together
item.description: As a physician who has spent years managing patients with persistent pain, I often encounter the same frustrating cycle. A patient arrives with unrele...

item.title: AI's a suck up. Research shows how it flatters and suggests we're not to blame
item.description: The AI models and chatbots that we interact with tend to affirm our feelings and viewpoints — more so than people do, with potentially worrisome conse...

item.title: How mosquitoes — and malaria — helped shape the whereabouts of early humankind
item.description: A new study looks at an unexpected force that played a critical role in shaping the lives of ancient humans.

item.title: Gradually, then suddenly: Dr. Robert Wachter on health care’s giant AI leap [PODCAST]
item.description: Subscribe to The Podcast by KevinMD. Watch on YouTube. Catch up on old episodes! What if the biggest problem with electronic health records was not th...

item.title: Challenges of Placebo Arms in Weight-Loss Drug Trials
item.description: Drugmakers continue to test weight-loss medicines against dummy pills and shots, even with a growing number of effective approved obesity drugs. This ...

item.title: Most Americans worried about brain health, but few know how to protect it
item.description: Nearly all Americans are worried about their brain health as they age, ranking as important or more so than their physical health, a new Alzheimer's A...

item.title: Lonapegsomatropin Shows Long-Term Benefit in Adult GHD
item.description: A 52-week extension study showed that the drug continues to maintain favorable body composition with no safety issues. Medscape Medical News

item.title: New targeted therapy shows promise against aggressive childhood and adult cancers
item.description: Researchers at the University of British Columbia Faculty of Medicine have developed a new targeted cancer therapy that can precisely seek out and des...

item.title: UK Biobank Members' Health Data Found for Sale on Alibaba
item.description: The UK government confirmed the breach after Biobank data on all 500,000 members appeared listed for sale, though names and addresses were not include...

item.title: Human cell model recreates Alzheimer's-linked tau pathology and synapse loss
item.description: Researchers at LMU have developed a human cell model that replicates key mechanisms of neurodegenerative diseases—with potential for novel therapies. ...

item.title: Why early detection technology and precision medicine are failing patients
item.description: The tools to detect serious disease years before symptoms appear already exist. What we are waiting on is a health care system structured to use them....

item.title: Increased childhood intake of ultra-processed foods is associated with a higher body mass index in young adulthood
item.description: 1. Greater childhood intake of ultra-processed foods was associated with higher body mass index in young adulthood. 2. The association was most eviden...

item.title: Transforming sepsis care with rapid host response diagnostics
item.description: In busy emergency departments, sepsis is often discussed as something we “cannot miss” rather than a diagnosis we can confidently make. Patients may a...

item.title: How research laboratory culture shapes mentorship in academic life
item.description: Every research mentor eventually faces a difficult question: What, and who, will continue when the work is no longer theirs to carry forward? We often...

item.title: Colorectal Cancer Predicted to Keep Rising in Younger Adults
item.description: Researchers say the rise in colorectal cancer among Americans younger than 45 is likely to keep accelerating over the next decade. Medscape Medical Ne...

item.title: Small-bites fascial closure reduces long-term risk of incisional hernia after midline laparotomy
item.description: 1. The small-bites fascial closure technique reduced the long-term incidence of incisional hernias compared to the traditional large-bites technique a...

item.title: TikTok “Fibermaxxing” trend targets gut microbiome health
item.description: 1. The viral “Fibermaxxing” trend prioritizes high-volume whole-food fiber intake to optimize gut microbiome diversity and improve metabolic satiety. ...

item.title: One-Month Limit Imposed on Ramipril 1.25-mg Supplies
item.description: New shortage protocol allows UK pharmacists to restrict dispensing as supply pressures hit a drug used by millions. Medscape News UK
